steps.title: Identify what would weaken the claim
steps.content: The students claim broken-wing display is “exclusive to shorebirds” (order Charadriiformes). To weaken this claim, data must show birds from other orders performing the display.

steps.title: Find non-shorebirds performing the display
steps.content: Choice D identifies the pine warbler (Passeriformes) and sooty grouse (Galliformes)—both performing the display and both outside Charadriiformes, directly undermining the exclusivity claim. Choices A and B don't show non-shorebirds performing the behavior, while C only demonstrates variation within shorebirds themselves. This matches choice D — D is correct.
